assertions.description: All 9 channels of the 9-channel Schmitt trigger design have R2(18K2)+C(100nF) RC low-pass filters correctly identified with fc=87.45 Hz (verified: 1/(2*pi*18200*100e-9) = 87.45 Hz). The analyzer correctly identified R2 as the filter resistor and excluded the upstream series resistor R1(82K) that is isolated by diode D1.

The 9-Schmitt design uses 74LVC3G14 (Triple Schmitt-Trigger Inverter ICs) with per-channel R-D-RC conditioning networks. All 9 subcircuit entries have description='' — the analyzer failed to classify the topology as 'Schmitt trigger input conditioning' or any equivalent label. Given that the part name 74LVC3G14 explicitly identifies it as a Schmitt trigger and the RC filter + series diode + series resistor pattern is a textbook Schmitt trigger input conditioning circuit, this is a missed classification opportunity.
